rules.rule_text:
title: DNS Query by Finger Utility
id: c082c2b0-525b-4dbc-9a26-a57dc4692074
related:
- id: 2fdaf50b-9fd5-449f-ba69-f17248119af6
  type: similar
- id: af491bca-e752-4b44-9c86-df5680533dbc
  type: similar
status: experimental
description: 'Detects DNS queries made by the finger utility, which can be abused
  by threat actors to retrieve remote commands for execution on Windows devices.

  In one ClickFix malware campaign, adversaries leveraged the finger protocol to fetch
  commands from a remote server.

  Since the finger utility is not commonly used in modern Windows environments, its
  presence already raises suspicion.

  Investigating such DNS queries can also help identify potential malicious infrastructure
  used by threat actors for command and control (C2) communication.

  '
references:
- https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/decades-old-finger-protocol-abused-in-clickfix-malware-attacks/
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
date: 2025-11-19
tags:
- attack.command-and-control
- attack.t1071.004
- attack.execution
- attack.t1059.003
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \finger.exe
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unlikely
level: high
